assertionResults.evidence: test_awq_dual_gemv.py: All numerical equivalence tests PASSED. max_abs_err=0.000209-0.000246 < 1e-2 threshold. Tested shapes: N=11008/13696/17408, K=5120, group_size=128. AWQ dual kernel produces output equivalent to two separate AWQ GEMVs + SiLU fusion.
assertionResults.consoleOutput:
VAL-AWQ-DUAL-001: ALL equivalence tests PASSED
  ✓ N=11008, K= 5120: max_abs_err=2.094209e-04
  ✓ N=13696, K= 5120: max_abs_err=2.033412e-04
  ✓ N=17408, K= 5120: max_abs_err=2.456307e-04

assertionResults.evidence: AWQ dual kernel selection confirmed via code inspection and kernel loading logs. Kernel loads: 'GEMV INT4 dual AWQ (fused gate+up+silu, no zero-point) loaded'. set_awq_mode(True) propagates to all 4 GPUs. However, cannot validate kernel selection in cached LaunchSpec without true AWQ model weights. With GPTQ weights, AWQ kernel mode causes NaN/Inf (expected: AWQ kernel skips zero-point subtraction, GPTQ weights have non-zero zeros). test_awq_dual_dispatch.py fails because it creates engine without loading weights.

metrics.blocked_by: No true AWQ model available (model at /opt/models/Qwen3.5-27B-AWQ is GPTQ format with qzeros)

assertionResults.consoleOutput:
Kernel loading: 'GEMV INT4 dual AWQ (fused gate+up+silu, no zero-point) loaded'
AWQ mode propagation: 'TPInferenceEngine.set_awq_mode(True) propagates to all 4 engines: True'
E2E with GPTQ weights: 'Step 0: NaN/Inf detected in output!' (expected - wrong weight format)

assertionResults.evidence: test_awq_dual_gemv.py: All throughput benchmarks PASSED. AWQ dual kernel shows 1.021-1.050x speedup over GPTQ dual kernel, exceeding 1.02x target. Zero-point arithmetic savings (8 v_sub_f32 per uint32 per projection) translate to measurable throughput improvement.
assertionResults.consoleOutput:
Throughput Report:
  ✓ N=11008, K= 5120: GPTQ=145.4us, AWQ=139.4us, speedup=1.043x
  ✓ N=13696, K= 5120: GPTQ=167.0us, AWQ=163.6us, speedup=1.021x
  ✓ N=17408, K= 5120: GPTQ=204.8us, AWQ=195.1us, speedup=1.050x

assertionResults.evidence: C dispatch integration code confirmed working: AWQ kernel mode enabled, dispatch cache rebuilds on mode change. However, E2E validation blocked by lack of true AWQ model. Running AWQ kernel mode with GPTQ weights causes NaN/Inf because AWQ kernel skips zero-point subtraction (assumes zeros=0), but GPTQ weights have non-zero zero points. Model at /opt/models/Qwen3.5-27B-AWQ is detected as 'gptq' format (has qzeros tensors).
assertionResults.consoleOutput:
AWQ mode: 'AWQ GEMV mode enabled: using gemv_int4_v5_awq_t16 + gemv_int4_dual_awq_fused (no zero-point)'
Dispatch: 'Dispatch cache built for 4 engines × 64 layers'
E2E GPTQ: 'Step 0: NaN/Inf detected in output!' (expected - AWQ kernel incompatible with GPTQ weights)

frictions.description: test_awq_dual_dispatch.py creates engine without loading weights, so layer cache is empty
frictions.suggestedFix: Test should either use mock weights or skip layer cache validation when weights not loaded

frictions.description: Model at /opt/models/Qwen3.5-27B-AWQ has qzeros tensors, detected as GPTQ format, not true AWQ
frictions.suggestedFix: Download true AWQ model (with qweight/scales but no qzeros) or create synthetic AWQ weights for testing

blockers.description: No true AWQ model available at /opt/models/. Model labeled 'AWQ' is actually GPTQ format (has qzeros). AWQ kernel mode with GPTQ weights produces NaN/Inf because AWQ kernel skips zero-point subtraction.
blockers.suggestedResolution: Download true AWQ Qwen 3.5 27B model from HuggingFace (e.g., from Qwen or casper-hansen) that has no qzeros tensors, or create synthetic AWQ weights for validation.

VAL-AWQ-001.summary: AWQ dual kernel produces numerically correct output (max_abs_err < 1e-2)

VAL-AWQ-002.summary: Kernel selection code correct, cannot validate E2E without AWQ model

VAL-AWQ-003.summary: AWQ dual kernel shows 2-5% speedup over GPTQ dual kernel

VAL-AWQ-004.summary: C dispatch integration code correct, cannot validate E2E without AWQ model
